variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_vowels && printf 'Hello World
AEIOU
xyz

Programming is fun
' > /tmp/eval_vowels/input.txt && printf '3
5
0
0
5
' > /tmp/eval_vowels/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_vowels/vowels.py — a Python program that reads lines from stdin and for each line writes the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u — both cases) on its own line to stdout. Empty lines yield 0.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_vowels && python3 vowels.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_vowels/vowels.rs — same I/O contract as the other variants (see Python/Go/C/C++ siblings). Idiomatic Rust 2021+. Will be compiled with `rustc -O vowels.rs -o vowels` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_vowels && rustc -O vowels.rs -o vowels && ./vowels < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_vowels/vowels.zig — same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`, `std.Io.File.stdin()` / `stdout()` for I/O, `takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns optional, null at EOF) or `appendRemainingUnlimited` to slurp stdin. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast vowels.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_vowels && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast vowels.zig && ./vowels < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
